observations.managementAction: Conduct of disposal is still on going.
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers are still in progress.

observations.recommendation: Facilitate the completion of the documents need for the request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.
observations.managementAction: Of the total ₱2.593 million dormant receivables, ₱1.082 million has been written off, while the write-off request for the remaining ₱1.511 million was returned, subject to the submission of additional documents.

observations.recommendation:
a. Exhaust all administrative legal means and cause the immediate transfer by making representation on the pending application with the DENR and locate/make representations on the claimants, to have the said property titled/transferred in the name of the Municipality; and
b. Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, direct the Municipal Accountant to book-up the lots based on the market/appraised value of the lots to fairly present the Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An independent professional has been hired to facilitate and accelerate the process.

observations.recommendation: Make representation with the Sanggunian Bayan to revisit and consider the possibility of revising the Municipal Tax Ordinance to provide the municipality with an opportunity to update market fees and charges in order to enhance collections and increase revenues.
observations.managementAction: No revisions were made in the Municipal Tax Ordinance.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Recognize the corresponding discounts granted for advance payments
under the Discount on Advance Payments account and credit the account
when the discount is applied;
b. Prepare the following adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of
the Discount on Advance Payments and RPT and SET receivable; and
Debit Credit
Discount on Advance Payments 88,402.66
Deferred RPT 39,290.07
Deferred SET 49,112.59
c. To avoid similar errors in the future, direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure proper segregation and classification of all components of real
property tax collections, including current dues, advance payments,
discounts, and penalties at the point of recording.

2.5 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Ensure that all donations of drugs and medicines are properly
documented and recorded in the books of accounts by the Municipal
Accountant in compliance with IPSAS 23, and that corresponding expenses
upon distribution and utilization;
b. Establish closed coordination with the Municipal Accountant, GSO, and RHU
to ensure that all future donations are formally reported, recorded, inspected
and accounted prior to distribution; and
c. Strengthen internal controls over the receipt and distribution of donated
goods to safeguard accountability, uphold management authority, and
preserve the legitimacy and transparency of public resource distribution.

3.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize and record the
depreciation allowance for the depreciable components of the Road
Networks and make the necessary adjustments to the accumulated
depreciation and book values of prior-year Road Network projects, in
compliance with IPSAS 17; and
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer promptly provide the Municipal
Accountant with monthly updates on all ongoing and completed projects,
including the actual dates of completion and acceptance, to ensure timely
and accurate recognition of depreciation.

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to drop the semi-expendable properties with cost below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold and other personal properties recorded in the RPCPPE; and
b. Direct the Property and Accounting Units to reconcile their records and to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in the Property Cards (PCs) and the PPE Ledger Cards and to submit an updated RPCPPE.
c. Direct the Inventory Committee to be guided by the procedures provided in COA Circular No, 2020-006 relevant to timely planning in the conduct of complete physical inventory count of all PPEs in accordance with the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), and to submit the same to the Office of the Audit Team Leader to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

a. Review its manpower complement in the Municipal Accountant’s Office, assess their existing workload, and if necessary, provide additional personnel and working space to cope with the increasing demands of work and expedite the submission of the financial reports; and
b. Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on the timely submission of financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate timely auditorial review.
Identify the contractors who were overpaid and immediately initiate steps to collect/recover the amount overpaid to the contractors concerned;

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) properly classified per Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) type and reconcile the discrepancy between the balances of PPE with the property records on various properties and the accounting records, and effect necessary adjustments, if necessary;
observations.reason: Reconciliation of property and accounting records is still properly ongoing.

observations.recommendation: b. Ensure that the submitted Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) is fully filled-out with all the relevant and required information for the damaged/unserviceable PPE and initiate disposal in accordance with applicable rules and regulations on Supply and Property Management and effect the necessary adjustments from the books of the Municipality;
observations.reason: IIRUP for December 31, 2023 not submitted and unserviceable not yet disposed.

observations.recommendation: c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and maintain an updated Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the required regular reconciliation of the PPE accounts as required under Sections 114 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
observations.reason: Property Cards and Ledger Cards not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation: d. Direct the Inventory Committee to be guided by the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 relevant to timely planning in the conduct of complete physical inventory count of all PPEs in accordance with the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), and to submit the same to the Office of the Audit Team Leader to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Physical Inventory was initially conducted. However, relevant to timely reconciliation/ Cleansing of PPE accounts per property and accounting records is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: a. Identify the contractors who were overpaid and immediately initiate steps to collect/recover the amount overpaid to the contractors concerned;
observations.reason: Amount overpaid to contractors not yet fully recovered/ collected.

observations.recommendation: a. Exhaust all administrative legal means and cause the immediate transfer by making representation on the pending application with the DENR and locate/make representations on the claimants, to have the said property titled/transferred in the name of the Municipality; and
observations.reason: The titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality is in process. Constant follow-ups were also made to fast tract the processing of the application in the DENR.

observations.recommendation: b. Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, direct the Municipal Accountant to book-up based on the market/appraised value of the lots to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality is in process. An independent professional has been hired to facilitate and expedite the process. Once the Torrens Title is finalized, municipal records and financial statements will be updated accordingly.

observations.recommendation: Make representation with the Sanggunian Bayan to revisit and consider the possibility of revising the Municipal Tax Ordinance to provide the municipality with an opportunity to update market fees and charges in order to enhance collections and increase revenues.
observations.reason: No revisions were made in the Municipal Tax Ordinance.

observations.recommendation: a. Request for write-off from the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected and those that are considered unaccounted; and
observations.reason: Request for write-off was submitted but returned without action due to additional documents that needs to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: b. Upon approval of request for write-off from the Commission on Audit, prepare the necessary adjusting/ correcting entries in the books of the Municipality to fairly present the receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Request for write-off with complete supporting documents was not yet submitted/returned.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to drop the semi-expendable properties
with cost below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold and other personal
property recorded in the RPCPPE; and
b. Direct the Property and Accounting Units to reconcile their records to
ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in the
Property Cards (PCs) and the PPE Ledger Cards and to submit an updated
RPCPPE.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the IIRUP for all obsolete or
unserviceable PPEs which is to be used for dropping from the books;
b. Direct the GSO to initiate and undertake the necessary process of disposal
of the duly accounted unserviceable properties following the updated
procedures prescribed in COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 dated
January 30, 2024; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary entries for the
dropping of these assets from the books of accounts, once the disposal
process is complete.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the unsubmitted vouchers
and lacking documentary requirements;
b. Direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the details in the Contract
Agreement are correct and the date of receipt of the Contractor on all
documents is filled-up to ensure compliance with the terms and conditions
of the contract; and
c. Direct all concerned officials/employees to process disbursements vouchers
only with complete supporting documents and with the approval of
proper/authorized officials to establish its validity/propriety, otherwise, the
transaction shall be suspended/ disallowed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Immediately initiate steps to collect/recover the amount overpaid to the
contractors concerned;
b. Hold the Municipal Accountant jointly liable for the amount not recouped,
if not refunded; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain subsidiary
ledgers by contractor and monitor closely all the payments made to avoid
the recurrence of overpayment to contractors beyond the contract cost.

We recommended and the Management agreed to facilitate the completion of
the documents need for the request for authority to write-off the dormant
accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Review its manpower complement in the Municipal Accountant’s Office,
assess their existing workload, and if necessary, provide additional personnel
and working space to cope with the increasing demands of work and expedite
the submission of the financial reports;
b. Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules and
regulations on the timely submission of financial reports and records of
receipts and disbursements to facilitate timely auditorial review.
a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized programs/projects/activities (PPAs) under development

b. Submit approved time extensions or suspension orders, if any, for the projects that incurred delays in the completion, otherwise liquidated damages should be imposed and collected from the contractor;

c. Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) to prepare specific projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan that partakes the nature of investments and capital expenditure in the 20 percent DF. Also, include specific and identifiable PPAs that have undergone priority ranking procedure in terms of urgency and impact;

d. Review the utilization of funds appropriated for PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, direct the LDC to realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs in accordance with the socio-economic development, environmental management and disaster response requirements provided in DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and

e. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its intended beneficiaries.

a. Submit approved Request for Time Extension or Suspension Orders, if any, for projects that incurred delays of completion, otherwise, impose liquidated damages and enforce collections from the contractor;

b. Require the contractor with significant delays incurred in the completion of the infrastructure projects to pay the liquidated damages and/or deduct the amount from the final payment or any succeeding claims on the project;

c. Ensure that all infrastructure contracts of the Local Government Unit (LGU) incurring delays in project completion be imposed with liquidated damages equivalent to 1/10 of one percent of the cost of the unfinished portion of the project for each day of delay;
d. Consider rescinding the contract if delays were incurred without valid justifiable cause and impose sanctions against the contractor to safeguard the interest of the LGU; and

e. Henceforth, strictly supervise and enforce the timely and full completion of the project within the target contract duration and conduct inspection of work accomplishment or adapt the punch listing on the date the projects are to be completed as agreed to determine the cost of unfinished portion of the project, if any.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain subsidiary ledgers by contractor and monitor closely all the payments made to avoid the recurrence of overpayment to contractors beyond the contract cost; and
c. Henceforth, strictly enforce the provisions of the Contract Agreement executed by and between the Municipality of Dumarao and the winning bidder.
a. Enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting more extensive tax campaigns and by strictly implementing the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of R.A. No. 7160;
b. Direct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate, update and reconcile their respective records to generate a reliable and accurate assessment record/report;

c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the updated and accurate data on RPT Assessment which will serve as basis for RPT target and collection every year on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the payment of delinquencies for RPT/SET as direct credit to RPT/SET Income account;
e. Allocate the collection received for the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property to SEF and immediately transfer to SEF; and

f. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of records to establish a realistic balance of Receivables from Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes so that necessary adjustment(s) in the books of accounts can be effected for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

b. Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, direct the Municipal Accountant to book-up based on the market/appraised value of the lots to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)-Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality is in-process.

observations.recommendation: b. Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) to prepare and specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan that partakes the nature of investments and capital expenditure in the 20 percent DF. Also, include specific and identifiable PPAs that have undergone priority ranking procedure in terms of urgency and impact which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality;
observations.reason: Some projects were presented in lumpsum and were described in generic terms with no project details, such as specific project name, cost, location or schedule of implementation, and expected output.

observations.recommendation: c. Make representation with the Sanggunian Bayan to revisit and consider the possibility of revising the Ordinance to provide the municipality with an opportunity to update market fees and charges in order to enhance collections and increase revenues.
observations.reason: No revisions were made in the Municipal Tax Ordinance to provide the municipality with an opportunity to update market fees and charges in order to enhance collections and increase revenues.

observations.recommendation: c. Request for write-off from the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected and those that are considered unaccounted; and
observations.reason: No request for account write-off was submitted.

observations.recommendation: d. Upon approval of request for write-off from the Commission on Audit, prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books of the Municipality to fairly present the receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: No request for write-off was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Prepare and submit RPCPPE and cause the reconciliation with the property
records and the accounting records, determine the respective costs of those
properties, and effect adjustments, if necessary;
b. Prepare IIRUP for the damaged/ unserviceable PPE and dispose these in
accordance with applicable rules and regulations on Supply and Property
Management and effect the necessary adjustments from the books of the
Municipality;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to
prepare and maintain an updated Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, to facilitate the required regular reconciliation of the PPE
accounts as required under Sections 114 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for
LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Direct the Inventory Committee to timely plan the conduct of thorough and
complete physical inventory count of all PPEs in accordance with the
approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), and submit the same to the Office of
the Audit Team Leader to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the
identified and prioritized development PPAs funded by the 20 percent DF, so
that any cause of suspension or problems that would delay the project
implementation would be immediately addressed and more desired benefits
could be delivered to the constituents;
b. Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) to prepare and specifically
describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan that partakes
the nature of investments and capital expenditure in the 20 percent DF. Also,
include specific and identifiable PPAs that have undergone priority ranking
procedure in terms of urgency and impact which are attainable within the
desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds and have an
immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality;
c. Ensure that every Purchase Order is fully filled-up with all the relevant and
required information to ensure faithful performance and compliance with the
terms and conditions of the contract, and impose liquidated damages in case
of delayed deliveries.
d. Review the utilization of funds appropriated for PPAs and ascertain viability of
implementation, otherwise, direct the LDC to realign or reprogram for more
viable and significant PPAs in accordance with the socio-economic
development, environmental management and disaster response requirements
provided by DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, DILG-
DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG and DBM JMC No. 01
dated March 27, 2020; and
e. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1
dated November 4, 2020 and DILG and DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020
in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF in order to achieve the
maximum benefits for its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to be vigilant in imposing liquidated damages
against the contractor due to the significant delay incurred in the completion
of the infrastructure projects in its succeeding claims;
b. Submit approved Request for Time Extension or Suspension Orders, if any, for
the projects that incurred delays of completion, otherwise, liquidated damages
should be imposed and collected from the contractor;
c. Consider rescinding the contract if delays were incurred without valid
justifiable cause and impose sanctions against the contractor for the best
interest of the Municipality; and
d. Henceforth, strictly supervise and enforce the timely and full completion of the
project within the target contract duration.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Evaluate and ensure compliance to the requirements set under the alternative
modes of procurement to promote efficiency in the implementation of projects
and desired benefits could be delivered to the constituents;
b. Direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the date of receipt of the Contractor is
filled-up to ensure compliance with the terms and conditions of the contract,
and right computation on liquidated damages may be imposed in case of
delayed completion; and
c. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No.
9184 in the procurement of goods and services.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to prepare and
include specific program, projects and activities that partakes the nature of
investments and capital expenditures in the Annual Investment Plan pursuant
to Article 454 (d) of Rules and Regulations Implementing the LGC;
b. Secure approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to
implementation of specific programs/projects/activities funded out of lump sum
appropriation pursuant to Article 4 (d) of the Rules and Regulation
Implementing the LGC and Item 3.1.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 as clarified in
COA Memorandum 2010-014 dated April 22, 2010; and
c. Henceforth, ensure that the funds appropriated for PPAs are optimally utilized
to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes of the Municipality.

a. Exhaust all administrative means and cause the immediate transfer by making representation on the pending application with the DENR and locate/make representations on the claimants, to have the said property titled/transferred in the name of the Municipality; and

observations.recommendation: a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development projects/Activities (PPAs) funded by the 20 percent DF, so that any cause of suspension or problems that would delay the project implementation would be immediately addressed and more desired benefits could be delivered to the constituents;
observations.managementAction: Some priority development projects for CY 2021 were either partially implemented or still on-going as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: c. Ensure that every Purchase Order is fully filled-up with all the relevant and required information to ensure faithful performance and compliance with the terms and conditions of the contract, and impose liquidated damages in case of delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized portion from the generic PPAs that were partially implemented and savings from completed projects was not used for other PPAs, reprogrammed or realigned for COVID-19 related expenses.

observations.recommendation: d. Review the utilization of funds appropriated for PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, direct the LDC to realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs in accordance with the socio-economic development, environmental management and disaster response requirements provided by DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG and DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020; and

e. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG and DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Low implementation rate for current year's 20% DF was recorded.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct the GAD Focal Person to properly plan, closely monitor, and strictly implement Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded by the 5% Gender and Development Fund and cause/recommend measures to realign to priority activities that will enhance the COVID-19 response activity of the Municipality so that problems/issues may be immediately addressed;
b. Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 in formulating PPAs in order to address gender issues and achieve maximum benefits for its constituents

Demand letters were sent to delinquent market stallholders, yet, some were still not able to make payment.

Amendments on some provisions of the Municipal Tax Ordinance No. 12-001 were made, however, some provisions were not strictly enforced and updated.

observations.recommendation:
Intensify efforts to enforce collection remedies provided by Republic Act No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order to demonstrate the Municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions; and 

Conduct more extensive tax campaigns so that taxpayers are apprised of the advantages/services the Municipality render if taxes are paid.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent and tax campaigns were conducted at the beginning of the year. However, operations and focus were shifted to disaster response to address COVID-19 situation.
observations.reason: Municipality’s operation is mainly directed to disaster response on COVID-19 emergency.

Some priority development projects for CY 2020 were either partially or not implemented.

Unimplemented PPAs were not reprogrammed but were scheduled to be implemented in the ensuing year.

Utilization of 20% Development Fund was not fully maximized.

Reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation:
Management advise the Administrative Officers to perform proper review prior to approval of  Loan Applications of employees under their jurisdiction, making sure that after amortizations of the loan applied for are deducted, the same will not bring the Net Take Home Pay of the employee to below the minimum required by the law; and



The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, in signing the Certificate of Net Pay of employees and upon determining that the Net Take Home Pay will fall below what is required by law, include a phrase stating that the same is not applicable for loan purposes, or any other similarly applicable notes, in order to strictly implement related provisions and restrictions of the law.
observations.managementAction: Proper and strict review of loan application was made, however, some loan application were approved even though net take home pay will fall below ₱5,000.00 due to considerations on employees’ financial needs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Review the lease contract on rates of rental and make the necessary action/amendment to align with the Municipal Tax Ordinance;
Exert more effort in collecting delinquent rental fees and issue notices of demand for rental payments inclusive of penalties from all delinquent market stallholders as soon as the payment falls due in accordance with its power to create sources of revenue; and
Henceforth, strictly implement the pertinent provisions of Municipal Tax Ordinance          No. 12-001 and Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended that Management:
Intensify efforts to enforce collection remedies provided by Republic Act No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order to demonstrate the Municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions; and
Conduct more extensive tax campaigns so that taxpayers are apprised of the advantages/services the Municipality render if taxes are paid.

We recommended that Management:
Attach serially numbered and duly accomplished driver’s trip ticket to support payment made for the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants;
Require Statement of Account/Bill from the supplier showing the date and total number of liters purchased as well as the price per liter and the total amount due for payment in order to verify the correct amount payable; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975 and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Expedite the execution of 20% Development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan so that more desirable benefits could be delivered to the constituents as planned;
Review unimplemented programs and projects and ascertain the validity of implementation, otherwise, reprogram for a more relevant and significant PPA as provided by DILG and DBM Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that:
Management advise the Administrative Officers to perform proper review prior to approval of  Loan Applications of employees under their jurisdiction, making sure that after amortizations of the loan applied for are deducted, the same will not bring the Net Take Home Pay of the employee to below the minimum required by the law; and
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, in signing the Certificate of Net Pay of employees and upon determining that the Net Take Home Pay will fall below what is required by law, include a phrase stating that the same is not applicable for loan purposes, or any other similarly applicable notes, in order to strictly implement related provisions and restrictions of the law.

a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the financial reports which are already overdue; and 

b.	Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume 1 on the timely submission of financial reports to facilitate timely auditorial review.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means, such as constant follow up to fast tract the processing of application in the DENR, to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality so that ownership thereof is fully established for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the immediate liquidation of cash advances within the time frame required by law;
Monitor the cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with unsettled accounts pursuant to Item No. 4 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Henceforth, strictly observe the prescribed timelines on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that Management:
Exert efforts in the reconstruction of records, verification, validation and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts to effect the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books of the Municipality;
Enforce collection by issuing follow-up demand letters to Hog Dispersal beneficiaries; and
Request for write-off from the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected.

We recommended that the Management:
a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the financial reports which are already overdue; and
b.	Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume 1 on the timely submission of financial reports to facilitate timely auditorial review.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means, such as constant follow up to fast tract the processing of application in the DENR, to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality so that ownership thereof is fully established for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Exhaust all legal means, such as constant follow up to fast tract the processing of the application in the DENR, to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality;
Appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens Title for the LGU’s real estate;  and
Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, prepare the necessary entries to book-up the market/appraised value in the books of the municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the immediate liquidation of cash advances within the time frame required by law. Likewise, Management should strictly observe the prescribed timelines on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means, such as constant follow up to fast tract the processing of application in the DENR, to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality so that ownership thereof is fully established for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

Strictly implement all the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget; and

See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues may be immediately addressed.

We recommended that Management exert efforts in verification, validation and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts to effect the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a. Establish a Municipal Council for the Protection of Children;
b. Allocate at least one percent of their IRA for Programs, Projects and Activities for
children’s welfare; and
c. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations about the formulation and utilization
of the one percent LCPC fund.

We recommended that Management:
a. Exert efforts in the reconstruction of records, verification, validation and analysis of
the dormant receivable accounts to effect the necessary adjusting/correcting entries
in the books of the Municipality;
b. Enforce collection by issuing follow up demand letters to Hog Dispersal beneficiaries;
and
c. Request for write-off for account balances that can no longer be collected in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the immediate liquidation of
cash advances within the time frame required by law. Likewise, Management should
strictly observe the prescribed timelines on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means, such as constant
follow up to fast tract the processing of application in the DENR, to have the said
property titled in the name of the Municipality so that ownership thereof is fully
established for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous
implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by municipality
under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Require the Sanggunian Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens Title
for the LGU’s real estate; and
c. Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, the Municipal Accountant should prepare
the necessary entries to book-up the market/appraised value in the books of the
municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land
and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly implement all the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget; and
b. See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that
problems/issues may be immediately addressed.

a. Submit justification on the issues raised in the preceding paragraphs; and
b. Investigate the practice of repeatedly engaging the same set of suppliers over and over again and take appropriate actions to rule out the possibility of supplier to supplier and/or supplier to agency personnel collusion.

a. Impose strictly the penal sanctions on violators of RA 9003, with the proper issuance of Citation Tickets, to yield increase in revenue and effectively maintain cleanliness in the Municipality; and
b. Indicate in the tarpaulin or billboard the penal sanctions for violators of the Municipal Ordinance.
observations.managementAction: a. Management had requested for printing of environment citation ticket and amendments of Ordinance No. 04-003 Section V to increase penalties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management ensure the submission of the Report on the Source and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the immediate liquidation of cash advances within the time frame required by law.

observations.managementAction: b.5 Management had coordinated with the PNP for the smoke-free ordinance implementation but Task Force is not organized yet due to insufficient personnel.

observations.recommendation:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the MOA and other related documents as required under Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review; and
b. Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to Leagues of Municipalities prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the immediate liquidation of
cash advances within the time frame required by law.

We recommended that Management exert efforts in the verification, validation and
analysis of the dormant receivable accounts to effect the necessary adjusting/correcting
entries in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a. Take the initiative in requesting the source agency on the immediate release of MOA
in order to dispatch the needed action for the immediate implementation of the
project;
b. Once the MOA is made available, effect full and timely implementation of the project
wherein the conduct of competitive bidding will have legal ground;
c. Direct the Municipal Engineer to notify the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal
Accountant on the failure to implement the project so that appropriate actions may be
instituted; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return/remit the
total amount allocated for the subject project to the source agency, if the program is
no longer feasible or could not be implemented.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the MOA and other related documents as required under
Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review; and
b. Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the
aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to Leagues of
Municipalities prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vive Mayor, as
the case maybe.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit justification on the issues raised in the preceding paragraphs; and
b. Investigate the practice of repeatedly engaging the same set of suppliers over and
over again and take appropriate actions to rule out the possibility of supplier to
supplier and/or supplier to agency personnel collusion.

We recommended that Management:
a. Impose strictly the penal sanctions on violators of RA No. 9003, with the proper
issuance of Citation Tickets, to yield increase in revenue and effectively maintain
cleanliness in the Municipality; and
b. Indicate in the tarpaulin or billboard the penal sanctions for violators of the Municipal
Ordinance.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management ensure the submission of the
Report on Source and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team duly certified by the
Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to
Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 17 (a) and (b) of RA No. 7160;
b. Address the aforementioned observations thru the following remedial measures:
b.1 Allocate budget for the procurement of one (1) unit of service vehicle and one (1)
microscope for mobile clinic;
b.2 Strictly implement the ordinance on Traditional Hilot. Review and revise
provisions which are no longer applicable;
b.3 Revise and strictly implement Responsible Pet Ownership ordinance. MAO to
regularly conduct effective dog vaccination. If no vaccine, allocate budget for the
procurement of anti-rabies vaccine;
b.4 The free anti-rabies vaccine is only available in animal bite center at
Sen. Gerardo M. Roxas Memorial District Hospital at Dao. If vaccine is available
at the RHU, this will encourage animal bite cases to seek early consultation and
will be properly managed; and
b.5 Strict implementation of Smoke-free Ordinance. Smoke Free Task Force should
also be organized.

a. Initiate the payment of salaries and employees’ allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Section 4(8) and 2 of PD 1445, with the expected result of minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the effects of manpower attrition; b. Make representation/arrangement with any government bank like the LBP or the PVB for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and c. Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA to strengthen fiscal management.
observations.managementAction: a.The management had initiated the adaptation of payroll-thru-bank scheme. b. The OIC-HRMO hand in the letter of the intent of the Municipal Mayor dated October 13, 2016 to avail of the ATM Payroll together with the SB Resolution No. 16-058 dated October 19, 2016. c. The MOA was already sent to the Management for signature. The Land Bank of the Philippines will coordinate with the Human Resource Officer for the conduct of AMLA Briefing, mechanics of Know-your-Client procedure and document requirements, which are all embodied in the MOA. Also, in anticipation of the replacement of ATM Cards to EMV (Euro-pay, Master Cards, Visa) on the second quarter of the year, issuance of the cards will be after the conversion.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct the personnel in charge of preparing the monthly payrolls to effect adjustments on deductions so that the monthly net take home requirement are not lower than P3,000.00 pursuant to the provisions of Section 48 of the GAA of FY 2015; and b. Inform the personnel concerned to make proper arrangement with the lending institutions for the adjustment of employees’ loan repayments.

a. Execute lease contract as well as contract for Build-Operate and Transfer (BOT); b. See to it that responsibility and accountability of both parties are duly observed; c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to demand rental payments inclusive of penalties as soon as payment falls due; d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make proper adjustment in the books to transfer the deposits made by lessees in the general fund; and e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record receivables for overdue payment on market stalls inclusive of penalties in the books as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: a.The Management is still working on it. b. Responsibility and accountability of both parties are duly observed; c. Demand Letters were already sent to the market stall occupants and collections for the unpaid rental with penalties are almost collected. d. Fully implemented. e. The receivable accounts amounting to ₱122,527.74 is recorded in the book of accounts.
